failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
Combined Loan-to-Value 80% exceeds maximum 75% for DSCR Investment: FICO Score 720+, up to $350K
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Occupancy 'Investment Property' not eligible. Available: Primary Residence
Super Jumbo program only available for Primary (Owner Occupied) occupancy - Non-Owner Occupied not eligible
Loan-to-Value 80% exceeds matrix limit 70% for FICO Score 800, Investment Property, $168,000, DSCR 1.25
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
DSCR income documentation is not eligible for Flex Select — use a DSCR program (e.g. DSCR Supreme, Investor DSCR) - FAIL
DSCR income documentation is not eligible for Flex Supreme — use a DSCR program (e.g. DSCR Supreme, Investor DSCR) - FAIL
DSCR Multi (5-8 Units): Only Residential 5-8 Units and Mixed Use 2-8 Units are eligible
Loan-to-Value 80% exceeds maximum 75% for FICO Score 740, Loan up to $1M: 75% Purchase/R&T, 65% Cash-Out
DSCR tier "DSCR > 1.15%" is too high - No Ratio program requires DSCR < 0.75 - FAIL

specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from Experian, TransUnion, and Equifax
Must include credit scores from all three bureaus
Must show complete tradeline details including payment history and account status
Must show mortgage history if any existing mortgages
Must include public record searches for each city where borrower resided in last 2 years
Must identify all inquiries, collections, charge-offs, disputes, and fraud alerts
Must be dated within 120 days of note date
Must verify borrower's Social Security number
Must certify all credit references provided on the loan application
reasons_needed: Credit score of 800 must be verified to confirm program eligibility for DSCR loan
Tradeline requirements (3 tradelines 12+ months OR 2 tradelines 24+ months) must be verified
Housing history and any existing mortgage payment history must be reviewed
Credit report is required to confirm absence of credit events (BK, FC, SS) for clean pricing
Public records and collections must be identified for eligibility determination
Tri-merge credit report is required per NQMF underwriting guidelines for all borrowers
document_requests.source_guideline: NQMF Credit Report Requirements - Tri-merge RMCR required for all borrowers

specifications: Must be FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) for 1-unit SFR property
Must identify subject property address matching 2345 Investment Property Rd, Nashville, TN
Must identify property type as Single-Family Residence and confirm 1-unit count
Must show appraised value to support $210,000 purchase price and 80% LTV calculation
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date (or within 365 days with 1004D recertification)
Must include all pages and addenda including comparable sales grid
Must include street map showing location of subject and all comparables used
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan with GLA calculation
Must include original color photographs: front, street, rear views of subject property
Must include interior photos: kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area, any physical deterioration
Must confirm property condition is C4 or better (C5/C6 ineligible; C4 requires marketability evaluation for DSCR)
Must confirm property is free of deferred maintenance as required for DSCR properties
Must confirm no fair or poor ratings, no health or safety issues
Must confirm property has minimum 400 square feet and contains full kitchen and bathroom
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, or environmental hazards impacting marketability
Must confirm property usage represents highest and best use
Must confirm property is accessible and available for year-round use
Must confirm residential or commercial zoning (rural properties not permitted)
Must include analysis of all sales of subject property within prior 3 years
Must include Certification and Statement of Limiting Conditions signed by appraiser
Must include appraiser license/certification valid for Tennessee
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting all NQMF loans
LTV/CLTV eligibility (80% LTV) depends on appraised value confirmation
Property type and unit count affect DSCR program eligibility
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
DSCR properties must be free of deferred maintenance per guidelines
Property condition C4 or better required; C5/C6 ineligible
Form 1004 is required form for 1-unit residential properties per NQMF guidelines

specifications: Must be a valid, unexpired government-issued photo identification
Must show borrower full legal name matching loan application
Must include photo, date of birth, and expiration date
Acceptable forms: US Passport, State Driver License, State ID Card, Permanent Resident Card

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity must be verified before credit, title, and compliance documents can be validated
Required for PATRIOT Act and CIP compliance

specifications: Must be fully executed with borrower wet or electronic signature
Must include correct borrower name, SSN, and current address
Must specify tax years for transcript request (most recent 2 years)
Must be dated within 120 days of closing

reasons_needed.reason: Required for all loan programs to authorize IRS tax transcript retrieval
Enables verification of income sources and tax filing status even for DSCR loans

specifications: Must certify property will be held as investment/rental property
Must confirm borrower will not occupy the subject property as primary residence or second home
Must be signed and dated by all borrowers
Must acknowledge investment property pricing and LTV restrictions apply

reasons_needed.reason: Occupancy type is investment property which requires explicit certification for DSCR program eligibility
Investment occupancy affects LTV limits (80% max), reserve requirements, and DSCR qualification methodology

specifications: Must be fully executed with all buyer and seller signatures
Must include subject property address matching appraisal: Tennessee SFR
Must show purchase price consistent with loan amount ($168,000) and 80% LTV
Must include earnest money deposit amount and disposition
Must include all addenda, amendments, and counteroffers
Must show closing date and contingency deadlines

reasons_needed.reason: Purchase transaction requires executed contract to validate purchase price, terms, and parties
Contract terms must be reviewed for seller concessions, non-arm's length indicators, and assignment clauses

specifications: Must identify all borrowers on the loan (Emmanuel Nyarko)
Must identify the subject investment property in Tennessee
Must state that the property is for commercial business or investment purpose only
Must be signed and dated by all borrowers
Must confirm property will NOT be occupied by borrower, any LLC member, or family member
Must be consistent with investment/non-owner-occupied occupancy declaration
Must reconcile with 1003 application and occupancy certification
reasons_needed: Required on all DSCR loans per guideline: 'All DSCR loans must be Business Purpose and processed as non-TRID transactions'
Guidelines mandate: 'Business Purpose & Occupancy Affidavit is required on all Business Purpose loans'
Documents borrower attestation that funds are used solely for business/investment purposes
Confirms non-TRID transaction status for regulatory compliance
Property must not be occupied by borrower or family members per business purpose requirements
document_requests.trigger: DSCR/Investor program type requires business purpose documentation
document_requests.cross_cutting_dedup: Occupancy Certification already requested in STEP_01 - Business Purpose Affidavit is separate requirement

specifications: Must cover 12 months of payment history on all existing mortgages
Must show 0x30 payment history (no 30-day lates in past 12 months)
Must identify property address and mortgage servicer for each mortgage
Must show payment due dates and actual payment dates
May be satisfied by credit report if mortgage tradelines are fully reported
If credit report shows complete mortgage history with 0x30x12, no separate documentation required
reasons_needed: Housing history verification is required for all NQMF programs
Mortgage payment history affects credit event determination and pricing
12-month 0x30 housing history is required for DSCR program eligibility
Any late payments may trigger additional seasoning requirements or pricing adjustments
conditions: Required only if borrower has existing mortgages (owned properties or liabilities showing mortgages)
May be waived if credit report shows complete 12-month mortgage history with 0x30 status
document_requests.source_guideline: NQMF Housing History Requirements - All programs require verification of borrower's housing history

specifications: Must be FNMA Form 1007 Single Family Comparable Rent Schedule
Must identify the subject property address matching the appraisal
Must include appraiser's opinion of market rent for the subject property
Must include analysis of comparable rental properties to support market rent estimate
Must be signed and dated by the appraiser
Must be completed in conjunction with the appraisal report
reasons_needed: DSCR ratio calculation requires gross market rent from Form 1007 for purchase transactions
For purchase transactions, appraiser's opinion of market rent on Form 1007 establishes qualifying rental income
DSCR Ratio = Gross Rental Income / PITIA - Form 1007 provides the gross rental income component
Guideline requires 1007/1025 for all Investor DSCR purchase transactions
document_requests.guideline_reference: DSCR RATIO / LONG TERM RENTALS - DSCR SUPREME AND INVESTOR DSCR

specifications: Must provide rent loss coverage for the subject property
Coverage amount must equal at least 6 months of PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must identify the subject property address
Must show coverage effective date and expiration date
Must show the insurer name and policy number
Blanket policies covering the subject property are eligible
Policy must be in effect at closing
Must provide Rent Loss Insurance coverage for the subject property
Must equal at least 6 months of PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must name property address: 2345 Investment Property Rd, Nashville, TN
May be included as endorsement on hazard policy or as separate coverage
Must be in effect prior to closing
Must include lender as loss payee or additional insured as applicable
reasons_needed: DSCR guidelines require Rent Loss Insurance for the subject property equal to at least 6 months of PITIA
Rent loss coverage protects against income loss if property becomes vacant
Required per DSCR Supreme and Investor DSCR program requirements
Blanket policies are acceptable provided the subject property is covered
NQMF guidelines require Rent Loss Insurance for DSCR loans equal to at least 6 months PITIA
Protects against income loss if property becomes uninhabitable
DSCR qualification depends on rental income; coverage ensures debt service protection
Required per Investor DSCR Program Matrix unless waiver option elected

specifications: Must provide most recent 2 consecutive months of statements
Must include all pages including blank pages
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name
Must show account number (full or masked acceptable)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each statement period
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per guideline requirements
Must cover all accounts being used for funds to close and reserves
Large deposits (exceeding 50% of qualifying income or unusual pattern) will require sourcing
reasons_needed: Required to verify sufficient funds for 20% down payment on $210,000 purchase ($42,000)
Required to verify funds for closing costs and prepaid items
Required to verify reserve requirements for DSCR investment property loan
Guidelines require assets be verified from acceptable sources
Account ownership must be confirmed to match borrower identity
document_requests.guideline_reference: ASSETS - Loan files must evidence sufficient funds from acceptable sources for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, debt payoff, and applicable reserves

specifications: Must provide copy of earnest money deposit check (front and back) showing clearing
Must show EMD amount matching purchase contract
Must show EMD sourced from verified borrower account
Must provide bank statement showing withdrawal of EMD funds
If wire transfer used, must provide wire confirmation receipt
Must reconcile to escrow receipt or HUD settlement statement
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD must be traced to acceptable borrower funds source
Ensures EMD credited properly at closing toward down payment
Required for funds to close reconciliation
document_requests.guideline_reference: ASSETS - funds from acceptable sources for down payment

specifications: Must include Fannie Mae SSR (Submission Summary Report) for the appraisal
Must show SSR Score of 2.5 or less to satisfy appraisal review requirements
Must confirm successful UCDP submission with no critical warnings
Must identify subject property address matching loan file
Must reference the appraisal report document ID
If SSR score exceeds 2.5, alternative review product (CDA, Field Review, or Second Appraisal) may be required
reasons_needed: NQMF Appraisal Review Process requires secondary appraisal product to support value
SSR Score of 2.5 or less satisfies standard review requirement
UCDP submission validates appraisal data integrity and identifies potential issues
Ensures appraisal meets GSE data quality standards

specifications: Must be FNMA Form 3170 (1-4 Family Rider / Assignment of Rents)
Must identify subject property address: 2345 Investment Property Rd, Nashville, TN
Must be executed by all borrowers holding title
Must assign rents to lender as additional security for the mortgage
Must be recorded with the mortgage/deed of trust
Must include borrower's agreement to maintain property in rentable condition
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require Form 3170 for all Investment Properties
Assignment of rents provides lender additional security for DSCR loans
Rental income from subject property is basis for DSCR qualification
Standard requirement for investment property collateral documentation

specifications: Must be FEMA Standard Flood Hazard Determination Form (SFHDF)
Must identify subject property address: 2345 Investment Property Rd, Nashville, TN
Must clearly identify flood zone designation in Section B4
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) - Zone A or Zone V
Must indicate NFIP community participation status if in SFHA
Must identify if Coastal Barrier Resources System (CBRS) or Otherwise Protected Area (OPA) applies
Must be issued by FEMA-certified flood determination vendor
Must include Life of Loan monitoring if applicable
reasons_needed: NQMF requires Flood Certificate to determine if property is in flood zone
Flood zone determination required to establish flood insurance requirements
Properties in Flood Zone A or V require flood insurance and NFIP community participation
Appraisal report flood zone must be reconciled with official FEMA determination

specifications: Must protect against loss from fire and standard extended coverage perils
Must include coverage for: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm (including named storms), hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must provide claims settlement on replacement cost basis (actual cash value not acceptable)
Must name all persons holding title as named insured (Emmanuel Nyarko)
Must show property address: 2345 Investment Property Rd, Nashville, TN
Must include standard mortgagee clause (loss payable clause alone not acceptable)
Must show coverage amount adequate for replacement cost of improvements
Must be effective for at least 60 days after funding date
Must include policy number, insurance company name, agent information
Must include effective and expiration dates of coverage
Must include premium amount and deductible
Must be signed and dated by agent
Must require insurer to notify mortgagee before policy cancellation
reasons_needed: Hazard insurance required on all loans to protect collateral
Coverage must be replacement cost basis per NQMF guidelines
Mortgagee clause protects lender's security interest
Named insured must match title holders to ensure borrower rights
Required prior to closing to protect collateral from date of funding

specifications: Must identify all vested owners matching borrower Emmanuel Nyarko or acceptable vesting entity
Must include subject property address and full legal description for Tennessee SFR
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, encumbrances, and title exceptions
Must show 24-month chain of title history per NQM guidelines
Must show title insurer name, commitment number, and effective date
Must be dated within 90 days of closing or per investor tolerance
Must identify Schedule A requirements and Schedule B exceptions
Title insurer must be licensed and authorized in Tennessee
reasons_needed: Required to confirm ownership and insurable interest in subject property
Must verify clear first lien position for the new mortgage
24-month vesting history must be reviewed for inconsistencies or flipping activity per NQM guidelines
All liens, judgments, and exceptions affecting title must be identified and cleared prior to funding
Title exceptions must be evaluated against allowable exception criteria
document_requests.source_trigger: All transactions require title commitment for underwriting review

specifications: Must be recorded deed showing current ownership of subject property
Must identify grantor and grantee with vesting matching borrower name or entity
Must include full legal description matching title commitment
Must show recording information (book, page, instrument number, recording date)
Must reflect current vesting status (sole ownership, joint tenancy, tenants in common, etc.)
For purchase transaction, seller's vesting deed establishes chain of title
reasons_needed: Required to confirm seller has clear title to convey to borrower at closing
Vesting must be reconciled with borrower identity and transaction structure
Legal description must match title commitment and appraisal
Supports 24-month chain of title review per NQM underwriting guidelines
document_requests.source_trigger: All transactions require vesting deed to confirm property ownership

specifications: Must identify the subject property address
Must identify tenant name(s) and landlord/owner
Must show monthly rent amount
Must show lease term (start and end dates)
Must be fully executed (signed by all parties)
If property is currently tenant-occupied, 1007/1025 must reflect the terms of the current lease
Lease must have no less than 1 month remaining at time of closing if refinance - for purchase may be vacant
Single room occupancy or boarder leases are not permitted
reasons_needed: If purchase property is tenant-occupied, lease agreement is required to document existing rental income
Lease terms must be reflected on Form 1007/1025 for tenant-occupied properties
For purchase transactions, property may be vacant - lease only required if tenant-occupied
Guideline states if property is currently tenant occupied, the 1007/1025 must reflect the terms of the lease
document_requests.condition: Required only if subject property is tenant-occupied at time of purchase

specifications: Must show sufficient liquid assets remaining after funds to close
Must verify minimum reserve months required by DSCR program (typically 6-9 months PITIA)
Must show verified account balances supporting reserve calculation
Reserves must be in borrower name or joint with spouse
Investment property reserves calculated using full PITIA payment
Gift funds may NOT be used for reserve requirements per guidelines
reasons_needed: DSCR investment property loans require verified reserves post-closing
Reserve months vary by LTV, property type, and loan amount
Ensures borrower has adequate liquidity buffer for investment property obligations
Guidelines explicitly prohibit gift funds for reserves
document_requests.guideline_reference: ASSETS - applicable reserves; GIFT FUNDS - Gift funds may not be used to meet reserve requirements
